response.justification: Bogdan brings rare and valuable geospatial/navigation domain expertise through GISCollective and strong distributed systems experience from microservices work handling millions of requests/second. However, the role requires modern C++ as the primary language, and his last significant C++ work was in 2014-2015 (10 years ago)—his recent experience is JavaScript/TypeScript-focused. While his demonstrated ability to learn new technologies across diverse stacks is promising, there's a substantial gap between his current skill set and the core C++ requirements. Interview could assess willingness to return to systems programming and depth of fundamentals.
strengths: Built GISCollective, an event-driven geospatial data platform—rare domain expertise directly applicable to routing tile systems
Demonstrated distributed systems mastery: optimized microservices handling millions of HTTP requests/second with async I/O, caching, and real-time databases
Proven ability to master multiple languages and technologies across embedded, web, mobile, and backend; explicitly picks tools for the job rather than forcing one stack
concerns: Last significant C++ work was 2014-2015 (10 years ago); current stack is JavaScript/TypeScript with no modern C++ production experience listed
No production experience with navigation/routing systems or tile generation algorithms—geospatial expertise is in data collection platforms, not routing infrastructure
Recent role is full-stack web engineering; lacks demonstrated systems-level C++ work typical of modern routing tile pipelines
